title: Sound Effects & Audio Feedback
summary: Day 4 review unanimously identified missing audio as the #1 priority, directly impacting Juice (4/10), Addictiveness, and First Impression scores. This day adds a complete procedural audio system using the Web Audio API — gate placement clicks, wire connection zaps, simulation signal pulses, success jingles, and fail buzzes. A mute toggle ensures accessibility. Secondary: highlight compatible pins during wire drawing for better clarity.

tasks.description: Create js/audio.js — a procedural sound engine using Web Audio API. All sounds generated via oscillators/noise, no external files. Sounds: click (gate place), zap (wire connect), pulse (simulation tick), success jingle (level complete), fail buzz (incorrect circuit), delete sound (gate/wire removal). Include a global mute toggle and master volume.
acceptance_criteria: AudioEngine class initializes Web Audio context on first user interaction
playClick() — short percussive click for gate placement
playWireConnect() — electric zap for wire connections
playWireDisconnect() — softer removal sound
playSimPulse() — subtle tick during simulation row evaluation
playSuccess() — ascending 3-note jingle for level complete
playFail() — descending buzzy tone for incorrect circuit
playButtonClick() — UI button feedback sound
setMute(bool) and isMuted getter
No errors if AudioContext is not available

tasks.description: Integrate audio into game flow — trigger sounds from main.js, ui.js on gate add, wire connect, simulation events, level complete, and fail
acceptance_criteria: Gate placement triggers click sound
Wire completion triggers zap sound
Gate/wire deletion triggers disconnect sound
Each simulation row triggers pulse sound
Level completion triggers success jingle
Failed simulation triggers fail buzz
RUN button triggers button click sound
Audio.js loaded in index.html before main.js

tasks.description: Add mute toggle button to the gameplay screen info panel
acceptance_criteria: Mute button visible in gameplay UI (speaker icon)
Clicking toggles between muted/unmuted states
Visual indicator shows current state (🔊 vs 🔇)
Mute state persists in localStorage

tasks.description: Highlight compatible pins during wire drawing — when user starts drawing from an output pin, highlight all available input pins (and vice versa) with a green glow
acceptance_criteria: When drawing a wire from an output pin, all unconnected input pins glow green
When drawing from an input pin, all output pins glow green
Glow disappears when wire drawing is cancelled or completed
Does not interfere with existing hover highlight

testing_focus: All 6 sound types play correctly and at appropriate volume
Mute toggle works and persists across page reload
Audio context initializes properly on first interaction
Compatible pin highlighting during wire drawing
rollback_criteria: If any P0 bug is introduced, revert to previous commit
